response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, but the resume lacks strong alignment with the core C++ requirements and industrial automation focus of the role. While there is some C/C++ experience (e.g., National Instruments), the majority of the work is in high-level languages (TypeScript, Node.js, D) and web/mobile development, with no mention of STL, multi-threading, message brokers, or IEC 62443 standards. The security and cybersecurity angle is also underrepresented.
strengths: Strong software development background with experience in C++ (National Instruments)
Proven ability to work on high-performance, low-latency systems (Dlang, Asynchronous I/O)
Experience in architectural design and mentoring (e.g., setting up CI/CD, dev-ops infrastructure)
concerns: Minimal C++ experience in the last 5 years (last significant C++ role was in 2015)
No clear evidence of working with STL, multi-threading, or message-broker systems required for this role
Lacks industry-specific experience in automation machinery or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards
